title: Minimal BC-GPSM Workflow
code: set.seed(123) n <- 300 z1 <- rnorm(n) z2 <- rnorm(n) age <- 50 + 8 * z1 biomarker <- 0.6 * z2 + rnorm(n, sd = 0.5) severity <- 0.35 * z1 - 0.25 * z2 + rnorm(n, sd = 0.9) distance <- runif(n, -1, 1) prior_tx <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(0.1 * z1 - 0.1 * z2)) clinic <- factor(rbinom(n, 1, 0.45), labels = c("Rural", "Urban"))  score <- cbind(   A = 0,   B = 0.35 * (0.05 * (age - 50) - 0.20 * biomarker +     0.18 * severity + 0.20 * prior_tx),   C = 0.35 * (-0.02 * (age - 50) + 0.25 * biomarker -     0.18 * distance + 0.18 * (clinic == "Urban")) ) prob <- exp(score) / rowSums(exp(score)) trt <- apply(prob, 1, function(p) sample(c("A", "B", "C"), 1, prob = p))  dat <- data.frame(   trt = factor(trt, levels = c("A", "B", "C")),   age = age,   biomarker = biomarker,   severity = severity,   distance = distance,   prior_tx = prior_tx,   clinic = clinic ) dat$y <- 1 + 0.5 * (dat$trt == "B") + 1.0 * (dat$trt == "C") +   0.03 * age - 0.4 * biomarker + 0.25 * severity - 0.2 * distance +   0.3 * prior_tx + 0.2 * (clinic == "Urban") + rnorm(n)  head(dat) #>   trt      age   biomarker    severity   distance prior_tx clinic        y #> 1   C 45.51619  0.10786082 -0.93005868  0.4646435        1  Rural 2.007534 #> 2   C 48.15858 -0.46528686 -0.60457237  0.2194214        0  Urban 2.981276 #> 3   B 62.46967 -0.57978839  1.04981690 -0.5512557        1  Rural 4.221387 #> 4   A 50.56407 -1.38954178  1.76295297  0.8323420        0  Urban 4.803756 #> 5   C 51.03430  0.13289695  1.13069590  0.6055220        1  Rural 5.526894 #> 6   A 63.72052  0.09334041 -0.04463277 -0.3751828        0  Urban 3.457639

title: Minimal BC-GPSM Workflow
text: Use dr_gpsm() cross-fitted estimator. example, treatment used reference group, contrast labels BvA mean estimated mean potential outcome B minus estimated mean potential outcome . final analyses, use larger nboot 500 . smaller value keeps example quick run.
code: fit <- dr_gpsm(   data = dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:7,   outcome = 8,   gps_model = "logit",   outcome_model = "lm",   folds = 2,   nboot = 50 )  data.frame(   contrast = names(fit$estimate),   estimate = fit$estimate,   ci_lower = fit$ci_lower,   ci_upper = fit$ci_upper,   row.names = NULL ) #>   contrast  estimate   ci_lower ci_upper #> 1      BvA 0.3841538 0.09204874 0.560658 #> 2      CvA 1.0971292 0.85642357 1.374370 #> 3      CvB 0.7129754 0.42319271 1.012751

title: Minimal BC-GPSM Workflow
text: gps_pre_process() can fit full-sample diagnostic GPS model add gps_ loggps_ columns. diagnostic scores useful visualizing overlap; dr_gpsm() uses cross-fitted GPS values internally.
code: gps_dat <- gps_pre_process(   data = dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:7,   gps_model = "logit" )  gps_histogram(gps_dat, bins = 20)

title: Minimal BC-GPSM Workflow
text: Use balance_check_plot() compare pairwise covariate balance nearest-neighbor matching. plot reports absolute standardized mean differences; smaller values indicate better balance. example standardizes covariates matching variables different scales contribute comparably matching distance.  function can also use existing loggps_* columns. example, creating gps_dat :
code: balance_check_plot(   data = dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:7,   match_on = "covariates",   standardize = TRUE,   style = "love" ) balance_check_plot(   data = gps_dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:7,   match_on = "gps",   fit_gps = FALSE )

title: Minimal BC-GPSM Workflow
text: Start transparent models: gps_model = "logit" multinomial logistic GPS estimation; outcome_model = "lm" simple outcome-regression adjustment; treatment_ref set explicitly natural control group exists. compare sensitivity flexible nuisance models gbm, rf, gam sample size large enough support .

title: Minimal BC-GPSM Workflow
text: baseline logit + lm workflow require optional modeling stack. Install packages needed selected model tuning path: xgboost ranger provide additional GPS outcome-model backends: two backends experimental remain active testing. now, use sensitivity analyses report package versions model settings whenever results shared.
code: install.packages(c(   "gbm", "mgcv", "VGAM", "randomForest", "glmnet",   "caret", "ModelMetrics", "pROC", "foreach" )) install.packages(c("xgboost", "ranger"))

title: Minimal BC-GPSM Workflow
text: dr_gpsm() validates common problems fitting nuisance models: checks concern whether software can fit requested analysis. replace overlap, balance, model-adequacy diagnostics.

what: Bias Corrected Generalized Propensity Score Matching
title: Bias Corrected Generalized Propensity Score Matching
text: BC-GPSM implements bias-corrected generalized propensity score matching observational studies binary multi-valued treatments, optional doubly robust outcome adjustment. package intended causal-inference workflows users treatment variable, set baseline covariates, outcome, want pairwise treatment contrasts bootstrap confidence intervals.

title: Bias Corrected Generalized Propensity Score Matching
text: baseline gps_model = "logit" outcome_model = "lm" workflow available without installing optional modeling stack. Install extensions needed planned analysis: Flexible learners tuning integrations optional. XGBoost ranger GPS outcome backends still testing development currently treated experimental sensitivity analyses, default specifications.
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("Leo-LiuQiang/BC-GPSM") install.packages(c(   "gbm", "mgcv", "VGAM", "randomForest", "glmnet",   "caret", "ModelMetrics", "pROC", "foreach" ))  # Experimental backends; install only when testing these model choices. install.packages(c("xgboost", "ranger"))

title: Bias Corrected Generalized Propensity Score Matching
text: first real analysis, start gps_model = "logit" outcome_model = "lm", compare optional flexible nuisance models gbm, gam, rf sample size supports . XGBoost ranger remain experimental. Use larger nboot 500 final reporting.
code: library(BC.GPSM)  set.seed(123) n <- 300 z1 <- rnorm(n) z2 <- rnorm(n) age <- 50 + 8 * z1 biomarker <- 0.6 * z2 + rnorm(n, sd = 0.5) severity <- 0.35 * z1 - 0.25 * z2 + rnorm(n, sd = 0.9) distance <- runif(n, -1, 1) prior_tx <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(0.1 * z1 - 0.1 * z2)) clinic <- factor(rbinom(n, 1, 0.45), labels = c("Rural", "Urban"))  score <- cbind(   A = 0,   B = 0.35 * (0.05 * (age - 50) - 0.20 * biomarker +     0.18 * severity + 0.20 * prior_tx),   C = 0.35 * (-0.02 * (age - 50) + 0.25 * biomarker -     0.18 * distance + 0.18 * (clinic == "Urban")) ) prob <- exp(score) / rowSums(exp(score)) trt <- apply(prob, 1, function(p) sample(c("A", "B", "C"), 1, prob = p))  dat <- data.frame(   trt = factor(trt, levels = c("A", "B", "C")),   age = age,   biomarker = biomarker,   severity = severity,   distance = distance,   prior_tx = prior_tx,   clinic = clinic ) dat$y <- 1 + 0.5 * (dat$trt == "B") + 1.0 * (dat$trt == "C") +   0.03 * age - 0.4 * biomarker + 0.25 * severity - 0.2 * distance +   0.3 * prior_tx + 0.2 * (clinic == "Urban") + rnorm(n)  fit <- dr_gpsm(   data = dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:7,   outcome = 8,   gps_model = "logit",   outcome_model = "lm",   folds = 2,   nboot = 50 )  data.frame(   contrast = names(fit$estimate),   estimate = fit$estimate,   ci_lower = fit$ci_lower,   ci_upper = fit$ci_upper,   row.names = NULL )

title: Bias Corrected Generalized Propensity Score Matching
text: can also ask dr_gpsm() save diagnostic plot:
code: gps_dat <- gps_pre_process(   data = dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:7,   gps_model = "logit" )  gps_histogram(gps_dat) dr_gpsm(   data = dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:7,   outcome = 8,   gps_model = "logit",   outcome_model = "lm",   hist = TRUE,   hist_path = tempfile(fileext = ".pdf"),   nboot = 20 )

title: Bias Corrected Generalized Propensity Score Matching
text: Use balance_check_plot() compare pairwise covariate balance nearest-neighbor matching. function reports absolute standardized mean differences returns ggplot2 object. example standardizes covariates matching variables different scales contribute comparably matching distance.
code: balance_check_plot(   data = dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:7,   match_on = "covariates",   standardize = TRUE,   style = "love" )

title: Bias Corrected Generalized Propensity Score Matching
text: dr_gpsm() returns list : estimate: estimated pairwise average treatment effects; ci_lower: lower confidence limits; ci_upper: upper confidence limits. Contrast names use form BvA, meaning estimated mean potential outcome treatment B minus estimated mean potential outcome treatment .

title: Bias Corrected Generalized Propensity Score Matching
text: workflow four main steps: Estimate treatment probabilities, also called generalized propensity scores. Convert GPS log-ratio matching features relative reference treatment. Match observations across treatment groups. Estimate pairwise treatment effects, optionally using outcome-regression predictions doubly robust adjustment. manuscript contains full theoretical framework assumptions. README focuses package workflow function arguments users need first.

title: Bias Corrected Generalized Propensity Score Matching
text: Common input errors reported model fitting: Additional diagnostic guidance: GPS diagnostics show poor overlap, interpret affected contrasts caution. Optional learners checked selected. one unavailable, error message names package installation command. xgboost ranger backends active testing; keep primary analyses behavior better validated. flexible models slow, begin gps_model = "logit" outcome_model = "lm" adding tuning machine-learning models. treatment control group labels BvA, set treatment_ref explicitly.

title: Covariate balance check plot — balance_check_plot
text: Computes pairwise absolute standardized mean differences (SMDs) nearest-neighbor matching, returns Love-plot style balance plot. Matching can performed existing loggps_* columns, full-sample diagnostic GPS fit, directly covariates.

title: Covariate balance check plot — balance_check_plot
code: balance_check_plot(   data,   treatment,   covariate,   treatment_ref = NULL,   match_on = c("gps", "covariates"),   gps_model = c("logit", "gbm", "gam", "xgboost", "ranger"),   gps_params = NULL,   fit_gps = TRUE,   cov_distance = c("mahalanobis", "euclidean"),   standardize = FALSE,   ridge = 1e-08,   match_ratio = 1L,   threshold = 0.1,   style = c("love", "faceted"),   return_data = FALSE )

title: Covariate balance check plot — balance_check_plot
text: data Data frame containing treatment covariates. treatment Single numeric column index treatment. covariate Numeric vector covariate column indices check. treatment_ref Optional reference treatment level. NULL, last observed treatment level used GPS fit needed. match_on Matching scale: "gps" "covariates". gps_model GPS model used match_on = "gps" fit_gps = TRUE; one "logit", "gbm", "gam", "xgboost", "ranger". Flexible learners require optional packages; XGBoost ranger experimental. gps_params Optional named list model-specific GPS parameters. fit_gps Logical. TRUE match_on = "gps", fit full-sample diagnostic GPS model computing balance. FALSE, data must already contain finite loggps_* columns. cov_distance covariate matching: "mahalanobis" "euclidean". standardize TRUE, center scale covariate matching features matching. ridge Small ridge used Mahalanobis whitening. match_ratio Integer >= 1; number nearest neighbors per target arm. threshold Reference line acceptable absolute SMD. style Plot style. "love" aggregates across pairwise treatment contrasts taking maximum absolute SMD covariate sample; "faceted" shows pairwise treatment contrast separately. return_data TRUE, return list plot balance; otherwise return plot.

title: Covariate balance check plot — balance_check_plot
text: ggplot2 object, list plot balance table return_data = TRUE.

title: Covariate balance check plot — balance_check_plot
code: set.seed(1) n <- 75 z1 <- rnorm(n) z2 <- runif(n, -1, 1) dat <- data.frame(   trt = factor(rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 25)),   age = 50 + 10 * z1,   biomarker = z2,   severity = 0.5 * z1 - 0.3 * z2 + rnorm(n, sd = 0.7),   prior_tx = rbinom(n, 1, plogis(0.4 * z1)),   site = factor(sample(c("S1", "S2"), n, replace = TRUE)) )  p <- balance_check_plot(   data = dat,   treatment = 1,   covariate = 2:6,   match_on = "covariates",   style = "love" )  p

title: Create all-pairwise contrast matrix — build_contrast
text: Create -pairwise contrast matrix

title: Create all-pairwise contrast matrix — build_contrast
code: build_contrast(levels, ref = NULL)

title: Create all-pairwise contrast matrix — build_contrast
text: levels Character vector treatment levels ref Reference treatment level. NULL, last level used.

title: Create all-pairwise contrast matrix — build_contrast
text: contrast matrix size Choose(K, 2) x K entries -1, 0, 1

title: Create all-pairwise contrast matrix — build_contrast
code: build_contrast(c("A", "B", "C"), ref = "A") #>      A  B C #> BvA -1  1 0 #> CvA -1  0 1 #> CvB  0 -1 1 build_contrast(c("A", "B", "C")) #>      A B  C #> BvA -1 1  0 #> AvC  1 0 -1 #> BvC  0 1 -1

title: Doubly-robust GPS matching estimator — dr_gpsm
text: Implements doubly-robust generalized propensity score (GPS) matching estimator optional cross-fitting bootstrap inference. GPS can estimated via multinomial logistic regression, GBM, GAM, XGBoost, ranger; outcome models can fitted via LM, RF, GBM, GAM, XGBoost, ranger. Flexible learners tuning integrations require optional packages. XGBoost ranger backends experimental remain active testing.

title: Doubly-robust GPS matching estimator — dr_gpsm
code: dr_gpsm(   data,   treatment,   treatment_ref = NULL,   covariate,   outcome,   gps_model = c("logit", "gbm", "gam", "xgboost", "ranger"),   outcome_model = c("none", "lm", "rf", "gbm", "gam", "xgboost", "ranger"),   folds = 2,   fold_seed = 12345,   nboot = 500,   boot_weight = c("multinom", "exp"),   hist = FALSE,   hist_path = NULL,   gps_params = NULL,   outcome_params = NULL,   match_on = c("gps", "covariates"),   cov_distance = c("mahalanobis", "euclidean"),   standardize = FALSE,   match_ratio = 1L,   gps_tune = FALSE,   gps_tune_control = NULL,   gps_tune_grids = NULL,   gps_seed = 12345,   outcome_tune = FALSE,   outcome_tune_control = NULL,   outcome_tune_grids = NULL,   outcome_seed = 12345,   two_step_calibration = FALSE,   calib_shrinkage = 1 )

title: Doubly-robust GPS matching estimator — dr_gpsm
text: data Data frame treatment, covariates, outcome variables treatment Column index treatment variable treatment_ref Reference treatment level. NULL, last observed treatment level used. selected reference moved first factor level internally GPS log-ratio construction. covariate Numeric vector covariate column indices outcome Column index outcome variable gps_model Choice GPS model: 'logit', 'gbm', 'gam', 'xgboost', 'ranger'. choices except 'logit' require optional packages. outcome_model Choice outcome model: 'none','lm','rf','gbm','gam', 'xgboost', 'ranger'. Flexible learners require optional packages. folds Number folds cross-fitting (default = 2) fold_seed Integer seed fold assignment (default 12345). nboot Number bootstrap replications (default = 500) boot_weight Bootstrap reweighting scheme: 'multinom' 'exp'. hist Logical; TRUE save diagnostic full-sample GPS histogram hist_path File path (pdf/png/jpg) save histogram hist = TRUE gps_params Optional named list hyperparameters per GPS model outcome_params Optional named list hyperparameters per outcome model match_on 'gps' (default) 'covariates' cov_distance covariate matching: 'euclidean' 'mahalanobis' (default 'mahalanobis') standardize TRUE, center/scale covariate features matching (default FALSE) match_ratio Integer >= 1; number matches per unit per target group (default 1) gps_tune Logical; TRUE, enable optional caret tuning GPS ('logit','gbm'). gps_tune_control Optional caret::trainControl() GPS tuning; default 5-fold CV gps_tune_grids Optional named list GPS tuning grids (e.g., list(gbm = ..., logit = ...)) gps_seed Integer seed GPS tuning fitting (default 12345) outcome_tune Logical; TRUE, enable optional caret tuning outcome models ('rf','gbm'). outcome_tune_control Optional caret::trainControl() outcome tuning outcome_tune_grids Optional named list outcome tuning grids (e.g., list(rf = ..., gbm = ...)) outcome_seed Integer seed outcome tuning fitting (default 12345) two_step_calibration Logical; TRUE, apply two-step residual calibration using ridge GPS index V. calib_shrinkage Numeric [0,1]; shrinkage multiplier applied calibration step size gamma.

title: Doubly-robust GPS matching estimator — dr_gpsm
text: list components: estimate Named numeric vector doubly-robust ATE estimates contrast. ci_lower Named numeric vector lower 95% confidence bounds. ci_upper Named numeric vector upper 95% confidence bounds.

title: Doubly-robust GPS matching estimator — dr_gpsm
text: treatment, covariate, outcome arguments must identify distinct, named columns. Missing outcome values currently modeled produce error guidance remove impute fitting. outcome_model = "lm", binary outcomes encoded numerically 0/1. treatment arm must contain least folds observations least match_ratio potential donors. Invalid settings reported nuisance-model fitting actionable error message.

title: Doubly-robust GPS matching estimator — dr_gpsm
code: set.seed(1) n <- 75 dat <- data.frame(   trt = factor(rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 25)),   x1 = rnorm(n),   x2 = runif(n) ) dat$y <- 1 + 0.4 * (dat$trt == "B") + 0.8 * (dat$trt == "C") +   dat$x1 + rnorm(n, sd = 0.5)  fit <- dr_gpsm(   data = dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:3,   outcome = 4,   gps_model = "logit",   outcome_model = "lm",   folds = 2,   nboot = 10 )  fit$estimate #>       BvA       CvA       CvB  #> 0.5887164 0.9360901 0.3473737

title: Plot overlaid histograms of non-crossfitted diagnostic GPS — gps_histogram
text: data Data frame returned gps_pre_process() (must contain gps_ columns) bins Number histogram bins (default = 30) palette Character vector fill colors (recycled shorter number GPS columns) alpha Fill transparency (0-1, default = 0.4) overlapping areas eps Small value truncate probabilities (avoids Inf logit; default = 1e-6)

title: Plot overlaid histograms of non-crossfitted diagnostic GPS — gps_histogram
text: ggplot object showing overlaid histograms density curves GPS distributions

title: Plot overlaid histograms of non-crossfitted diagnostic GPS — gps_histogram
code: set.seed(1) n <- 75 dat <- data.frame(   trt = factor(rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 25)),   x1 = rnorm(n),   x2 = runif(n) )  gps_dat <- gps_pre_process(   data = dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:3,   gps_model = "logit" )  gps_histogram(gps_dat, bins = 10)

what: GPS- or Covariate-matching ATE estimator (numeric-column interface) — gps_matching
title: GPS- or Covariate-matching ATE estimator (numeric-column interface) — gps_matching
text: cross-fitted inference, use dr_gpsm(); gps_matching()'s bootstrap intended standalone (non-cross-fitted) usage.

title: GPS- or Covariate-matching ATE estimator (numeric-column interface) — gps_matching
code: gps_matching(   data,   treatment,   outcome,   pred,   contrast,   nboot = NULL,   match_on = c("gps", "covariates"),   covariate = NULL,   cov_distance = c("mahalanobis", "euclidean"),   standardize = FALSE,   ridge = 1e-08,   match_ratio = 1L,   return_tau = FALSE,   do_boot = FALSE,   boot_weight = c("multinom", "exp") )

title: GPS- or Covariate-matching ATE estimator (numeric-column interface) — gps_matching
text: data Data frame treatment Column index treatment variable (factor) outcome Column index outcome variable pred pred K x n matrix m_k(X) predictions aligned rows data contrast C(K, 2) x K contrast matrix build_contrast() nboot Number bootstrap replications (used do_boot=TRUE; can NULL otherwise) match_on 'gps' (default) 'covariates' covariate Numeric vector covariate column indices (required match_on='covariates') cov_distance 'euclidean' 'mahalanobis' (match_on='covariates') standardize Logical; standardize covariate features matching ridge Small ridge covariance Mahalanobis whitening match_ratio Integer >= 1; number matches per unit per target group return_tau Logical; TRUE, also return tau (tau_centered) do_boot Logical; TRUE, compute bootstrap CI within fold; FALSE, skip (ci_* = NA) boot_weight Bootstrap reweighting scheme: 'multinom' 'exp' (used do_boot=TRUE).

title: GPS- or Covariate-matching ATE estimator (numeric-column interface) — gps_matching
text: list(estimate, ci_lower, ci_upper, tau?, tau_centered?)

title: GPS- or Covariate-matching ATE estimator (numeric-column interface) — gps_matching
code: set.seed(1) n <- 75 dat <- data.frame(   trt = factor(rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = 25)),   x1 = rnorm(n),   x2 = runif(n) ) dat$y <- 1 + 0.4 * (dat$trt == "B") + 0.8 * (dat$trt == "C") +   dat$x1 + rnorm(n, sd = 0.5)  gps_dat <- gps_pre_process(   dat,   treatment = 1,   treatment_ref = "A",   covariate = 2:3,   gps_model = "logit" ) contrast <- build_contrast(levels(gps_dat$trt), ref = "A") arm_means <- tapply(gps_dat$y, gps_dat$trt, mean)[levels(gps_dat$trt)] pred <- matrix(rep(as.numeric(arm_means), each = nrow(gps_dat)),   nrow = length(arm_means),   byrow = TRUE )  gps_matching(   data = gps_dat,   treatment = 1,   outcome = 4,   pred = pred,   contrast = contrast,   do_boot = FALSE ) #> $estimate #>       BvA       CvA       CvB  #> 0.3063032 0.8779711 0.5716679  #>  #> $ci_lower #> BvA CvA CvB  #>  NA  NA  NA  #>  #> $ci_upper #> BvA CvA CvB  #>  NA  NA  NA  #>

what: Generalized Propensity Score (GPS) Pre-processing (with optional GPS fitting) — gps_pre_process
title: Generalized Propensity Score (GPS) Pre-processing (with optional GPS fitting) — gps_pre_process
text: cross-fitted BC-GPSM workflow, function primarily: enforce treatment factor + reference level; keep complete cases (treatment + covariates); ALWAYS create gps_ loggps_ placeholder columns (NA default); optionally fit full-sample GPS model (diagnostic , e.g., histogram).

title: Generalized Propensity Score (GPS) Pre-processing (with optional GPS fitting) — gps_pre_process
code: gps_pre_process(   data,   treatment,   treatment_ref = NULL,   covariate,   gps_model = c("logit", "gbm", "gam", "xgboost", "ranger"),   gps_params = NULL,   tune = FALSE,   tune_control = NULL,   tune_grids = NULL,   seed = 12345,   fit_gps = TRUE )

title: Generalized Propensity Score (GPS) Pre-processing (with optional GPS fitting) — gps_pre_process
text: data data.frame containing treatment/covariates (possibly outcome). treatment Single numeric column index treatment. treatment_ref Optional reference level. NULL, last observed treatment level used. selected reference moved first factor level internally log-GPS ratios use denominator. covariate Numeric vector covariate column indices. gps_model One "logit","gbm","gam","xgboost","ranger" (used fit_gps=TRUE). Flexible learners require optional packages; XGBoost ranger backends experimental active testing. gps_params Optional named list model-specific parameters. tune Logical; TRUE, optional caret tuning used "logit" "gbm" fit_gps=TRUE. tune_control Optional caret::trainControl() object tuning. tune_grids Optional tuning grids list. seed Integer seed. fit_gps Logical; FALSE, fit GPS; return placeholders .

title: Generalized Propensity Score (GPS) Pre-processing (with optional GPS fitting) — gps_pre_process
text: data.frame original columns + gps_ loggps_ (always present).

title: Generalized Propensity Score (GPS) Pre-processing (with optional GPS fitting) — gps_pre_process

title: Fit PS model on training fold and predict probabilities on eval fold — pmatch_fit_ps_fold
code: pmatch_fit_ps_fold(   train_df,   eval_df,   t_name,   x_names,   model = c("logit", "gbm", "gam", "xgboost", "ranger"),   tune = FALSE,   tune_control = NULL,   tune_grid = NULL,   gps_params = NULL,   tr_levels = NULL,   seed = 12345 )

title: Fit PS model on training fold and predict probabilities on eval fold — pmatch_fit_ps_fold
text: train_df training data frame eval_df evaluation data frame t_name treatment column name (factor) x_names covariate column names model "logit","gbm","gam","xgboost","ranger". Flexible learners require optional packages; XGBoost ranger experimental. tune Logical; optional caret tuning logit/gbm. tune_control caret::trainControl() NULL. tune_grid named list components logit/gbm (style gps_tune_grids) gps_params optional named list model params (style gps_params) tr_levels full set treatment levels enforce (character) seed integer seed
